Description: Accounting staff arrived to find every spreadsheet and invoice replaced with encrypted files and a ransom note demanding payment.
Sales representatives cannot access customer records because shared drives are completely unavailable.
Unable to prepare or submit customer bids because the estimating application will not open and project files are inaccessible.
Several employees reported payroll direct deposit information was changed without their knowledge.
Nearly every virtual server is displaying ransom messages after an overnight outage.
Forklift scheduling computers repeatedly reboot and display unknown error messages.
Company email appears to be sending messages that nobody in the organization wrote.
Multiple vendors reported receiving fraudulent payment instructions that appear to originate from our company.
Employees cannot log into customer support software because accounts appear to have been locked or disabled.
Design documents have disappeared from shared folders overnight.
Production has stopped because equipment management software cannot communicate with workstations.
Corporate website has been replaced with an unauthorized message.
Bank notified us of several outgoing wire transfers that nobody on the finance team recognizes.
Confidential contract documents appear to have been downloaded by an unknown account.
CRM records for active customers are missing and sales staff cannot contact prospects.
Endpoint protection software has stopped reporting from hundreds of company computers.
Employees are reporting pop-up windows demanding payment before files can be recovered.
Employee records containing personal information appear to have been copied outside the organization.
Every project drawing now has an unfamiliar file extension and cannot be opened.
Helpdesk agents are receiving hundreds of password reset requests from users who cannot access their accounts.
Accounts payable cannot process invoices because financial systems are completely unavailable.
Customers are calling to report suspicious emails requesting payment to unfamiliar bank accounts.
Shipping has stopped because inventory data is no longer available.
Multiple engineers report their laptops becoming extremely slow before suddenly locking them out.
Major contract deadline will be missed because proposal documents cannot be recovered.
Backups scheduled overnight did not complete and recent recovery points are unavailable.
Customer database cannot be accessed and incoming support requests are piling up.
Employees report unauthorized purchases appearing on company purchasing accounts.
Business operations are largely halted because critical applications are unavailable across multiple departments.
Large numbers of workstations are inaccessible, encrypted, or repeatedly displaying ransom notifications after reboot.
